channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: The Mysterious Rolls-Royce Accident: Collins Mnangagwa involved in “Ginimbi-style” Rolls-Royce crash soon after his Belarus trip
item.description: Harare – The streets of Harare are rife with speculation and unanswered questions following a horrific car crash involving Collins Mnangagwa, son of President Emmerson Mnangagwa. The incident, which occurred on 27 June 2026, left a luxury Rolls-Royce in a mangled wreck, its wheel eerily “plucked off” in a manner strikingly similar to the fatal [...] The post The Mysterious Rolls-Royce Accident: Collins Mnangagwa involved in “Ginimbi-style” Rolls-Royce crash soon after his Belarus trip first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: 4-wicket Nyamhuri puts Zimbabwe in charge of Bangladesh Test
item.description: Unbeaten 76 from Kaia lifts Zimbabwe to 136 for the loss of one wicket as they chase down Bangladesh total of 140 The post 4-wicket Nyamhuri puts Zimbabwe in charge of Bangladesh Test appeared first on Zimbabwe News Now. (via Zimbabwean News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwean News

item.title: Zimbabwe’s Mbinga: Sir Wicknell Chivayo’s Controversial Empire Expands Globally... From Harare to Cape Town, Eswatini & Nairobi @wicknellchivayo
item.description: CAPE TOWN – The man who has become a symbol of ostentatious wealth in Southern Africa has done it again. Wicknell Chivayo, the flamboyant businessman known to his followers as “Sir Wicknell”, has recently taken to social media to flaunt his latest acquisition: a R160 million (approximately US$8.8 million) mansion nestled in the exclusive Atlantic [...] The post Zimbabwe’s Mbinga: Sir Wicknell Chivayo’s Controversial Empire Expands Globally... From Harare to Cape Town, Eswatini & Nairobi first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: The OK Zimbabwe Photo: What Happens When the Pavement Becomes the Marketplace?
item.description: Some photographs reveal more about an economy than official statistics. The now familiar images of informal traders operating outside an OK Zimbabwe supermarket have attracted considerable public attention. At one level, the scene appears entirely ordinary. Across Zimbabwe’s towns and cities, vendors have become a permanent feature of the urban landscape. Yet the significance of [...] The post The OK Zimbabwe Photo: What Happens When the Pavement Becomes the Marketplace? appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Quiet Money, Deep Roots: How the Oak Foundation Backs Zimbabwe’s Communities
item.description: The Geneva-based Oak Foundation has funded Zimbabwean causes since 1983. In 2025 its Zimbabwe programme made USD 7.9 million in grants, backing palliative care, landmine clearance, regenerative farming and care for children who have lost parents. #Zimbabwe #Policy #ZimbabwePolicy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tapiwa Hillary Phillipo
